evidence_errors: Schema validation failed at $.naming_pass: Additional properties are not allowed ('recomputed_concentration' was unexpected)
blocking_failures: Schema validation failed at $.naming_pass: Additional properties are not allowed ('recomputed_concentration' was unexpected)

hardness_axes.name: Discover
hardness_axes.reasoning: The instruction states only the symptoms (some catalogs select the wrong revisions or load too much) and the verification method; it never states the fold-pruning rule, the exclude-bump rule, the replace-brings-its-own-requirements rule, the maximum-of-minimums rule, or the retract-stays-but-held rule. The solver must reproduce the symptoms, study the model doc and the catalogs, and reconstruct all five rules before it can localize and correct the five wrong stages.

hardness_axes.name: Synthesize
hardness_axes.reasoning: A correct build list spans five interacting stages (requirement reading with pruning, replace substitution, exclude bumping, maximum-of-minimums selection, retract reporting) across the whole catalog; no single package, node, or doc holds the complete behavior, and the chosen revision of one node depends on decisions made several nodes away.

hardness_axes.name: Diagnose or Design/Search
hardness_axes.reasoning: Repair shape: the burden is diagnosing why the shipped planner is wrong from observable symptoms and the model, localizing the five defects among decoy packages, and correcting each, not implementing a blank-canvas spec.

hardness_axes.name: Navigate coupling
hardness_axes.reasoning: Local fixes break distant invariants: correcting the replace stage can pull in a folded node whose deeper requirement must then be pruned by the requirement stage; correcting the exclude stage can raise a revision that changes the maximum the selection stage must pick; the retract stage changes the preview without changing the selection, so a fix that conflates them regresses other catalogs.

hardness_axes.name: Reason beyond training
hardness_axes.reasoning: Minimal version selection (the build list is the maximum of the required minimums, not the lowest version) and module-graph pruning (a folded node contributes its direct requirements but not its deeper ones unless reached through an open node), combined with exclude-bump and retract-stays semantics, are famously misunderstood; the obvious whole-graph, lowest-revision mental model is exactly wrong and is what the simple bundled catalogs reward.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Disclosure-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Even if the instruction fully states the input format, the output schema, and that results must match the model, the residual work — pruning deep requirements behind folded nodes, bumping excluded revisions, substituting replaces with their own requirements, taking the maximum of minimums, and keeping retracted revisions selected while withholding them from the preview — remains a real engineering problem, not transcription.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hidden-instance
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Difficulty is not about guessing which bundled catalog is special; the verifier runs the solver against fresh held-out catalogs, so a general planner is required and finding one broken record is not enough.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Single-artifact repair
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no one line or toggle to flip; the five defects live in five packages and each requires substantive corrected logic, and the comprehensive and coupling catalogs fail unless several are correct together.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Generalization
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Held-out catalogs are authored and seed-generated at verify time with adversarial wiring, so a solver that targets only the bundled catalogs fails.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Prompt-honesty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: An honest prompt can state the schema, the run interface, and the symptoms without collapsing the search, because the search is the reconstruction and correction of the selection rules themselves, which the prompt does not spell out.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Cheating-vs-difficulty
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Held-out recomputation in an independent Python planner and fresh catalogs are anti-cheating measures; the legitimate difficulty is the selection logic itself, not the anti-cheating.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Mechanical-fix filter
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The task is not about verifier dependencies, timeouts, or reward formatting; it is the planner's build-list behavior under the four directive kinds and pruning.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Localized-fix
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The frontier is five separate stage packages across distinct directories, the instruction names none of them, and three decoy packages do plausible-looking off-path work, so the solver must locate and coordinate all five.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Oracle-locality
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The oracle rewrites five substantive stage bodies in five packages, not one whole-file overwrite of a short helper.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Small declarative-cluster
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The solve is a graph traversal with pruning, directive application, and a maximum-of-minimums fixed point, not one config block or a literal table mirroring the prompt.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Grep-collapse
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction nouns (module, version, require, exclude, replace, retract, prune, select, ...) do not grep to the opaque per-package `Step` functions in pass1-pass5; paths and symbols are noun-free and use a separate neutral catalog vocabulary.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Pre-factored-helper
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Helper packages render/tally/banner do diagnostic and formatting work and do not mirror the five selection concerns; the shipped stage bodies encode the obvious wrong rules rather than a near-correct helper the solver could lift.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Recipe-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: There is no standard recipe: pruned-graph traversal combined with exclude-bump, replace-requirements, maximum-of-minimums, and retract-stays is not a known one-liner and the obvious whole-graph lowest-revision approach is wrong.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Security-aura discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: No security framing is used as a stand-in for hardness; the residual reasoning is the selection semantics.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Orthogonal-checklist
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The five stages are coupled, not independent: replace can introduce a folded node whose deeper requirement the requirement stage must prune, exclude can raise the maximum the selection stage picks, and retract changes only the preview; changing one shifts the others' results.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Harness-discount
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: The Go module build, the bundled catalogs, and the run scripts are realism, not hardness; the hardness is the planner behavior.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: One-pass solvability
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: A frontier model that opens the files and assumes a whole-graph lowest-revision planner passes the simple bundled catalogs but fails the held-out pruning, exclude, replace, retract, and maximum cases; converging requires reasoning about coupling and re-running against generated catalogs.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Hard-only gate
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: This is clearly hard, not merely respectable: a plausible whole-graph lowest-revision planner looks correct on the visible catalogs yet is wrong on exactly the subtleties minimal version selection and pruning were designed to handle.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Discovery/design budget test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: At least five non-trivial discoveries (pruned direct-but-not-deeper traversal, exclude bump-to-next, replace contributes its own requirements, maximum-of-minimums selection, retract-stays-but-held) are forced from the model, the catalogs, and the symptoms — none stated as a recipe in the instruction, and none reducible to merely diffing code against a stated spec.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Instruction specificity test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Instruction is symptoms-only for a repair shape: it states the observable wrong behavior and the verification method while withholding the cause, the fix locations, and the selection rules.

anti_trivialization_checks.name: Topology distribution test
anti_trivialization_checks.reasoning: Three repair topologies each require coordinating at least three locations (pruning+replace+selection; exclude+selection+pruning; replace+exclude+retract); no single location passes the held-out subsets alone.

rubric_axes.name: Verifiable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An independent Python planner recomputes the expected build list, held set, and upgrade preview for each held-out catalog; comparisons are deterministic equality with seeded generation.

rubric_axes.name: Well-specified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Two engineers reading the instruction, the model doc, and the interface doc would build equivalent verifiers from the stated catalog grammar, run interface, output schema, and match-the-model contract.

rubric_axes.name: Solvable
rubric_axes.reasoning: An engineer who knows minimal version selection and module-graph pruning can solve it in a few hours; the reference fix is a few hundred lines of Go, well under 100k.

rubric_axes.name: Difficult
rubric_axes.reasoning: Requires real Go-modules expertise plus careful pruned-graph traversal and fixed-point reasoning; it is not solvable by tricks, a hidden fact, or a single obscure flag.

rubric_axes.name: Interesting
rubric_axes.reasoning: Reconstructing a build list under pruning and the four directive kinds is real toolchain work people are paid to do when building module proxies, vendoring tools, SBOM pipelines, and reproducible builds.

rubric_axes.name: Outcome-verified
rubric_axes.reasoning: Grading checks the build list, held set, and preview the solver's program emits on held-out catalogs; it does not dictate language, data structures, or algorithm and accepts any program that matches the model.

instruction_completeness_test.reasoning: Reading only instruction.md, an agent knows the input format, the run interface, the output schema, and the observable symptoms but not how pruning prunes, how excludes bump, how replaces contribute requirements, how selection takes the maximum, or how retracts behave; the model doc, the catalogs, and runtime behavior are essential, so the instruction is not a recipe.

discovery_budget.discovery: The build list keeps a folded node's own direct requirements but not the deeper requirements behind it, unless that deeper node is also reached through an open node; the shipped planner reads the whole transitive graph and so loads requirements a pruned graph would never see.
discovery_budget.planned_location: environment/pass1/pass1.go (requirement reading with fold pruning) and environment/docs/model.md
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Stating the pruning rule as steps would turn the most-misunderstood part of the model into transcription rather than diagnosis from the symptoms.

discovery_budget.discovery: A replace substitutes a required node (optionally only at a specific revision) with another node revision and the build must then read the replacement's own requirements, not the original's; the shipped planner rewrites the identity but keeps reading the original's requirements.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Revealing that the replacement contributes its own requirements would hand over the coupling that makes the replace cases hard.

discovery_budget.discovery: An excluded revision is not usable, so a requirement landing on it must bump up to the next available revision (skipping consecutive excludes); the shipped planner treats the excluded revision as usable.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Naming the bump-to-next rule would collapse the exclude cases into a lookup instead of a behavior the solver must reconstruct.

discovery_budget.discovery: Selection is the maximum of the required minimum revisions across the whole surviving graph (minimal version selection), not the lowest required revision; the shipped planner takes the minimum.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Spelling out that 'minimal' means the maximum of the minimums would remove the central reasoning that distinguishes correct selection from the naive lowest-revision approach.

discovery_budget.discovery: A retracted revision stays in the build list when it is the selected maximum and is only reported as held and skipped by the upgrade preview; the shipped planner drops retracted revisions out of the build list entirely.
discovery_budget.why_instruction_must_not_reveal: Disclosing that a retract does not change the selection would convert the retract cases into a checklist instead of inferred behavior.

instruction_specificity.reasoning: Repair shape: the instruction states the observable wrong behavior (some catalogs select the wrong revisions or load too much), the run interface, the catalog input, the output schema, and the verification method, while withholding the cause, the fix locations, and the five selection rules.

attack_path.summary: Reproduce the symptoms with the run and suite scripts, read the model doc and the catalogs, then correct each stage: read direct-but-not-deeper requirements behind a folded node, substitute replaces with their own requirements, bump excluded revisions to the next available, select the maximum of the minimums, and keep retracted revisions selected while holding them back from the preview; self-check against the illustrative outputs and fresh catalogs.
attack_path.reasoning: Each step constrains the others: pruning decides which requirements exist, replace decides which node and requirements contribute, exclude decides which revisions are usable, and selection takes the maximum over the survivors; the preview depends on the final selection. A competent engineer follows this path but must derive every rule from the model and the observable artifacts rather than from the instruction.

smallest_plausible_patch.summary: Rewrite the five stage bodies so the emitted build list, held set, and upgrade preview match the model on held-out catalogs, including pruning, exclude bumping, replace requirements, maximum-of-minimums selection, and retract-stays behavior.
smallest_plausible_patch.reasoning: There is no smaller patch: leaving any stage in its shipped form drops a whole class of behavior (deep requirements over-load, replaces miswire, excluded revisions get selected, the minimum is chosen, or retracted revisions vanish), so the genuine change is several hundred lines of coordinated traversal, substitution, and selection logic.

collapse_audit.reasoning: After full disclosure of the schema, the run interface, and the symptoms, the residual work is the correct implementation of pruning, exclude bumping, replace requirements, maximum-of-minimums selection, and retract reporting, recomputed against fresh held-out catalogs by an independent Python planner; no surface signal shortcuts it and the simple bundled catalogs actively mislead a naive planner into reading as correct.
collapse_audit.residual_hardness: Coordinating five coupled stages so the build list, held set, and preview match the model on unseen catalogs, where the obvious whole-graph lowest-revision approach is provably wrong on pruning, exclude, replace, retract, and maximum cases.
red_flags: RC6 may WARN because the output schema and the run interface are intentionally disclosed for actionability.
Empirical Part E difficulty is pending until frontier-agent runs are available; this evidence argues construction hardness, not the post-upload platform label.

topology_enumeration.summary: Whole-graph traversal corrected by adding pruning, then fixing selection, with replace folded in.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Pruning in pass1 and replace in pass2 decide which requirements exist; only then can pass4 take the correct maximum. Missing any one yields a build list that over-loads or selects the wrong revision.

topology_enumeration.summary: Directive-first repair where excludes and selection are corrected before pruning is trusted.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: Exclude bumping in pass3 and selection in pass4 bound which revisions win, but unless pass1 prunes the deeper requirements the build list still over-loads and the maximum is computed over phantom requirements.

topology_enumeration.summary: Report-and-substitute path that interleaves replace, exclude, and retract.
topology_enumeration.why_no_single_location_suffices: A replace in pass2 can target a revision that an exclude in pass3 must bump and that a retract in pass5 must report as held; handling only one leaks the wrong identity, the wrong revision, or the wrong held set.

symbol_table.purpose: reads each reachable node's direct requirements and, only when the node is open, its deeper requirements, so the graph stays pruned behind a folded node
applies the root-level substitutions, redirecting both a requirement's chosen identity and its requirement source to the substitute
bumps a required revision off a forbidden revision to the next available one, skipping consecutive forbidden revisions
sets each node's chosen revision to the maximum of its required minimum revisions
marks chosen revisions that are withdrawn as held and computes the upgrade preview that skips withdrawn revisions

decoy_manifest.non_fix_purpose: renders a human-readable indented tree of the chosen set for the diagnostic subcommand
tallies node and requirement counters for the suite summary line only
formats the catalog name as a header label for verbose console output off the selection path

renames_during_drafting.reason: a function named after the graph-pruning concept would telegraph the hardest stage and collide with instruction nouns; renamed to an opaque per-package Step.
a function named after the replace directive would mirror an instruction noun; renamed to opaque Step.
a function named after version selection would mirror instruction nouns; renamed to opaque Step.
the shared type named after the build graph would expose forbidden nouns on the fix-path signatures; renamed to an opaque State in the plan package.

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the bundled catalogs are simple — every node required at one revision, all nodes open, no excludes, replaces, or retracts — so the naive whole-graph lowest-revision planner reproduces their outputs exactly
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver validates against the visible outputs, sees them match, and concludes the planner is correct, missing the held-out cases the simple catalogs never exercise
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the instruction states the bundled outputs are illustrative and that the verifier runs additional held-out catalogs, and the interface doc states the simple catalogs do not pin down the rules

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the pruning rule, the exclude-bump rule, and the retract-stays rule live in the model doc and the catalog structure, not as a recipe in the instruction
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a solver that walks the whole graph and treats minimal as the lowest revision never prunes deep requirements or selects the maximum
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the conceptual model is documented in a readable doc the solver may study and the catalogs are small enough to inspect fully

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: a replace can introduce a folded node whose deeper requirement must then be pruned, an exclude can bump a revision that changes the maximum, and a retract changes the preview but not the selection
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: fixing one stage in isolation leaves the others wrong, and the coupling means a partial fix still fails the comprehensive and coupling catalogs
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the dependency between stages is observable by running the planner on catalogs that combine the directives and comparing

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: a planner that fixes four of five stages still produces output that round-trips through the schema and passes the bundled catalogs while failing held-out exclude, replace, or retract cases
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: schema-valid, sample-passing output reads as success; the shortfall only surfaces against the independent planner on fresh catalogs
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: the instruction states results are checked against the model on catalogs beyond the bundled data

difficulty_mechanism_plan.placement: the catalog text format, the run interface, the JSON output, the Go planner, and the independent Python planner must agree on the same selection semantics
difficulty_mechanism_plan.why_model_misses_it: a partial implementation handling one directive emits output that parses but disagrees with recomputation under the independent planner
difficulty_mechanism_plan.fairness_guardrail: every output key, the catalog grammar, and the run command are visible in the instruction or the docs

calibration_plan.human_sanity: an engineer who knows minimal version selection and module-graph pruning can solve it by reading the model doc, reproducing the symptoms, and correcting each stage: read direct-but-not-deeper requirements behind a folded node, substitute replaces with their own requirements, bump excluded revisions to the next available, select the maximum of the minimums, and keep retracted revisions selected while holding them back from the preview, self-checking against the illustrative outputs
calibration_plan.shortcut_audit: tests regenerate output by running the solver's program, build fresh held-out catalogs at verify time, reject static or hardcoded outputs, recompute expected values with an independent Python planner rather than trusting any bundled file, and include catalogs whose correct output contradicts the naive planner so sample-matching fails
calibration_plan.ablation_plan: revert each of the five stage bodies to its shipped form independently and confirm at least one held-out verifier test fails for that stage only

pass_rate_target.basis: stronger target agent; aligns with Part E Hard/Medium/Easy thresholds on worst/best model accuracy. The verifier is offline (pytest baked into the Dockerfile, no runtime installs under allow_internet = false) and post-upload difficulty is classified by Part E after platform agent runs.

metrics.criterion: the build list, held set, and upgrade preview match the independent planner on the bundled and held-out catalogs

subtype_milestone_plan.sidecar_or_protocol_notes: bundled data catalogs and the illustrative-output JSON are local development evidence; the verifier loads authored held-out catalogs and seed-generates additional ones at verify time, all offline

fix_frontier.distribution: five planner stages across five opaque packages: requirement reading with fold pruning, replace substitution, exclude bumping, maximum-of-minimums selection, retract reporting
fix_frontier.naming_policy: opaque package dirs pass1-pass5 each exposing one Step function; shared types live in a non-fix plan package and the pipeline driver references only the shared Step name
fix_frontier.helpers_policy: sibling helper packages render/tally/banner do diagnostic/formatting work that never touches the chosen revisions

task_shape.collapse_risk: medium; mitigated by an independent planner, verify-time-generated held-out catalogs, deliberately simple bundled catalogs, a non-textbook selection model, and a five-stage fix frontier

category_profile.challenge_family: repairing a minimal-version-selection planner whose pruned-graph traversal interacts with exclude, replace, and retract directives
category_profile.allowed_instruction_disclosures: the catalog input format, the run and suite commands, the three-key output schema, the offline expectation, the observable symptoms, and that the bundled outputs are illustrative
category_profile.forbidden_instruction_leaks: the fold-pruning rule, the exclude-bump rule, the replace-requirements rule, the maximum-of-minimums rule, the retract-stays rule, the stage identifiers, and per-node expected revisions
category_profile.category_specific_hardness_bar: the chosen revision of one node derives from pruning decisions, exclude bumps, replace substitutions, and the maximum over every surviving requirement; no single directive or node suffices and local choices change distant nodes' revisions
category_profile.category_specific_verifier_risks: trusting a tamperable bundled report, single-catalog tests, clean-only graphs with no excludes/replaces/retracts, hidden generated-file expectations, or accepting a tool that only reproduces the illustrative catalogs
category_profile.coverage_role: adds the first version-selection and module-graph-pruning task, distinct from the existing Cargo feature-resolution, cgroup allocation, and overlay-flatten tasks

satisfiability_risk.rc2_planned_name_risk: low: planner stages are opaque pass1-pass5, helpers are render/tally/banner, and data/doc paths use a neutral catalog vocabulary while instruction nouns stay off the fix path
satisfiability_risk.gx9_contract_risk: low: the instruction states the schema and points at illustrative outputs but no per-node answer key; expectations are recomputed by the independent planner on held-out catalogs
satisfiability_risk.cr1_symbol_frontier_risk: low: the manifest fixes the five Step symbols and the oracle modifies only those packages
satisfiability_risk.hidden_contract_risk: low: every tested input keyword, output key, and run command is in instruction.md or the docs

actionability_plan.verifier_command_visible: instruction.md names the run command (bash /app/environment/scripts/run.sh <catalog-dir>), the suite script, and the pytest verifier command
actionability_plan.source_fix_intent_visible: instruction.md says the planner sources under /app/environment (also reachable through /app) build and run but compute the wrong result and must be repaired, and that static output writes are insufficient
actionability_plan.generated_output_rule_visible: instruction.md identifies the JSON emitted on stdout and the suite report, and states catalogs beyond /app/environment/data are also planned at verify time
actionability_plan.exact_formula_home: the conceptual selection model lives in /app/environment/docs/model.md; the precise per-directive rules are inferable from the model, the catalogs, and the symptoms and enforced by held-out tests, not stated as a recipe
actionability_plan.schema_home: instruction.md names the three output keys and /app/environment/docs/interface.md documents the catalog grammar and shows one concrete example of the output shape

waiver_plan.waiver_rationale: no waiver is planned; any checker warning should be resolved or documented before packaging

reference_pattern.justification_if_none: Original task built from the real Go modules minimal-version-selection and module-graph-pruning model (go.dev/ref/mod minimal-version-selection and graph-pruning sections, plus Russ Cox's MVS writeups), minimized into a local deterministic planner with a bundled offline catalog format; not derived from a website task-inspiration, a prior public task, or a reskin of the existing cargo-feature-unification, cgroup-budget-solver, or overlayfs-whiteout-flatten tasks. The reference library currently has no promoted entries, so no reference_task_id applies.

realism_source.evidence_basis: Go modules minimal version selection (the build list is the maximum of the required minimum versions), module graph pruning for go 1.17+ modules (a pruned graph keeps a module's own requirements but not the deep transitive requirements behind it unless reached through an unpruned module), and the exclude, replace, and retract directive semantics, as documented at go.dev/ref/mod
realism_source.upstream_or_synthetic_rationale: the selection and pruning semantics come directly from a real, widely-used toolchain; only the specific catalogs and the opaque planner scaffold are authored to keep the task local, offline, and deterministic
realism_source.minimization_preserves: maximum-of-minimums selection, pruned-graph direct-but-not-deeper traversal, exclude bumping to the next available revision, replace contributing the replacement's own requirements, and retract keeping a revision selected while withholding it from the upgrade preview
realism_source.synthetic_exception_review: not a synthetic exception; the model and rules are drawn from a real build toolchain and only the instances are authored
